name: Brad Evered at CrossCountry Mortgage
description_short: Brad Evered is a mortgage loan officer at CrossCountry Mortgage based in Seattle, WA, specializing in home purchase and refinance loans.
description_long:
Brad Evered operates as a mortgage loan officer serving the Seattle, Washington market through CrossCountry Mortgage, a national mortgage lender. CrossCountry Mortgage is an established player in the residential mortgage industry, offering financing solutions to homebuyers and existing homeowners seeking to refinance their properties. The company maintains a digital presence with individual loan officer profiles, allowing consumers to connect with specific originators in their geographic area.

As a loan officer at CrossCountry Mortgage, Brad Evered would typically facilitate mortgage applications, pre-qualification assessments, and loan processing for clients in the Pacific Northwest region. The company's website infrastructure suggests they offer standard mortgage products including home purchase loans and refinancing options, though the specific product details (FHA, VA, conventional, jumbo, reverse mortgages) are not clearly enumerated in the provided website content. Clients can access loan officer profiles to initiate contact and begin the mortgage application process.

CrossCountry Mortgage operates with modern digital infrastructure, including cookie consent management, security protocols (reCAPTCHA), and video consultation tools (Vidyard), suggesting an online-first customer experience model. The company implements industry-standard privacy and data protection measures, with detailed cookie policies and consent management systems in place. Their use of analytics and performance tracking tools indicates a focus on optimizing the customer journey through their platforms.

Without access to specific loan product details, rates, fees, reviews, or performance metrics from the website content provided, a comprehensive assessment of Brad Evered's competitive positioning is limited. Prospective borrowers should directly contact the loan officer or review independent rating sources to evaluate loan terms, customer service quality, and pricing compared to other mortgage lenders in the Seattle market.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Local loan officer presence in Seattle market for personalized service
National mortgage lender infrastructure suggests access to diverse loan products
Digital-first platform with video consultation tools (Vidyard) for remote applications
Industry-standard security protocols including bot detection and data encryption
Organized online presence with individual loan officer profiles for easy identification
cons: Limited product transparency on webpage—specific loan types and rates not disclosed
No customer reviews, testimonials, or ratings visible on the provided website content
Generic landing page with no competitive differentiation or unique value propositions outlined
No information on fees, APR ranges, or lending criteria available for comparison
Unclear application process or timeline details from the accessible website content
best_for: Seattle-area homebuyers seeking personalized local loan officer guidance
Homeowners in Washington state interested in refinancing existing mortgages
Borrowers comfortable with online application processes and video consultations
Consumers comparing mortgage options who want to work with an established national lender
diagnosis: Brad Evered at CrossCountry Mortgage is suitable for Seattle-area homebuyers and refinancing borrowers seeking a local loan officer with national lender backing. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency on specific loan products, rates, fees, and terms—direct contact with the loan officer or comparison shopping with competitors is essential before committing.
services: Loan officer direct contact and support
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
